text.title: recognition-over-recall Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for recognition-over-recall as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Icon-only toolbars where users must memorize icon meanings.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Icons without labels require recall of meaning. Even "universal" icons (save floppy disk, hamburger menu) are not universally recognized by all user populations.

failure-mode.description: Multi-step forms with no progress summary of prior selections.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-screen recall burden is the most common form of heuristic #6 violation and is invisible in static design reviews — requires prototype testing to detect.

failure-mode.description: Agent flags all icon-only elements including universally understood icons (magnifying glass for search, X for close).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provide agent a list of safe icon exceptions before running audit. Magnifying glass and X are recognized across all user populations and do not need labels.

failure-mode.description: Agent audits static wireframes and misses cross-screen recall failures.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Information visible on screen A but gone on screen B is a dynamic property. Note this limitation explicitly in audit output; require prototype testing to validate cross-screen context.
